data.title: Spetz announces name change to SonicStrategy
data.description:
• Spetz (OTCPK:DBKSF) announced official approval of its rebranding to reflect its focus as a dedicated validator infrastructure operator for the Sonic blockchain.
  • The company will file articles of amendment effective December 2, 2025 and complete its legal name change to "SonicStrategy Inc." 
  • In connection with the name change, the company will also change its ticker symbol on the Canadian Securities Exchange from "SPTZ" to "SONI".

data.title: Peter Thiel–backed drone maker Quantum Systems soars to €3B+ valuation with new €180M raise
data.description:
• Quantum Systems, a German drone maker backed by Peter Thiel, has raised €180 million in a Series C extension led by Balderton Capital, bringing its 2025 funding total to €340 million and tripling its valuation to more than €3 billion.
  • The funding represents the largest private raise in Europe’s dual-use sector this year, the company said.
  • The startup, whose new drone Jaeger is designed to intercept hostile unmanned aircraft, has seen demand surge following recent drone disruptions at major airports.
  • Quantum Systems was founded by former German army pilot Florian Seibel in 2015 to produce dual-use unmanned aerial vehicles. It’s backed by investors including Thiel, Porsche Automobil Holding SE (OTCPK:POAHY) and Notion Capital.

data.title: Petrobras trims five-year investment plan by 2% over low oil prices
data.description:
Brazilian state-controlled oil company Petrobras (PBR) trimmed its five-year investment plan for 2025-2029 by about 2%, reducing capital expenditures from $111 billion to $109 billion.

This move aims to protect its cash flow amid lower international oil prices. About $91 billion of the total will go to projects already under implementation, though $10 billion still requires budget confirmation pending financing analysis.

"Given the scenario of lower oil prices, the BP 2026-30 reinforces the commitment to growth with value generation and the company's financial sustainability through capital discipline, operational efficiency, optimization of operating expenses, and budget limits for investments, in addition to the adoption of more restrictive criteria in the governance for projects approvals" the company said in a statement.

The company is focused on maintaining financial stability without increasing debt or altering its dividend policy. This reduction to its multi-year plan is a first after President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva took office in 2023 and reflects balancing economic development goals with a challenging global oil price environment.

data.title: Germany's retail sales rise 0.9% Y/Y in October
data.description:
• Retail sales in Germany increased 0.90% in October over the same month in the previous year. 

  • Retail sales decreased 0.30% in October of 2025 over the previous month, above consensus of 0.20%.

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:EWG), (NYSE:GF), (NASDAQ:FGM), (NASDAQ:DAX), (NYSEARCA:FLGR) (DAX:IND)

  • Currency: (EUR:USD)

data.title: Germany set to approve €2.9 billion arms package as defense spending surges
data.description:
• German lawmakers are reportedly set to approve spending €2.9 billion ($3.4 billion) on 11 military procurement contracts, including for drones, rifles, and missiles, in deals that will go largely to domestic manufacturers.
  • The defense ministry asked parliament to give the green light for the orders, including the purchase of as many as 250,000 G95 assault rifles from Heckler & Koch for €765 million, according to procurement documents seen by Bloomberg News.
  • Lawmakers are expected to approve the purchases at a closed-door meeting next week.
  • According to earlier reports, Germany was looking to approve defense equipment purchases worth more than $3.47 billion, including 20 light combat helicopters from Airbus SE (OTCPK:EADSF).

data.title: Plantify Foods announces rights offering
data.description:
• Plantify Foods (PTFY:CA) intends to issue rights to holders of record of its common shares at 2:00 p.m. on December 5, 2025, for the purchase of up to a total of ~11.44M shares.
  • Pursuant to the rights offering, each shareholder of Plantify will receive one transferable right for every common share held as of the record date.
  • One right will entitle the holder thereof to subscribe for one common share at a subscription price of $0.12 per common shae on December 31, 2025.
  • Assuming the exercise of all rights, the rights offering will raise gross proceeds of ~$1.37M.

data.title: Dream Industrial REIT names operations chief
data.description:
Dream Industrial REIT (DIR.UN:CA) appointed Gord Wadley as its COO, effective January 1, 2026.

Mr. Wadley will oversee all aspects of portfolio operations, including property management and leasing.

He is currently the COO of Dream Office REIT and has been with the Dream organization since 2011, with a strong background in commercial brokerage and real estate leadership.

data.title: Navitas Semiconductor signs distribution agreement with WT Microelectronics
data.description:
• Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS) consolidated its Asian distribution network by signing a strategic distribution partnership with WT Microelectronics.
  • This partnership enhances technical and supply chain services for Navitas's leading Gallium Nitride and Silicon Carbide power devices in Asia. 
  • Under the collaboration, WT Microelectronics will lead customer engagement, design-in activities, and regional logistics to ensure reliable product availability and fast delivery of Navitas products across Asia.

data.title: CME halts trading due to cooling issue at data centers
data.description:
The derivatives exchange operator CME Group (CME) halted trading across its markets on November 28, 2025, due to a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers.

"Due to a cooling issue at CyrusOne data centers, our markets are currently halted. Support is working to resolve the issue in the near term and will advise clients of Pre-Open details as soon as they are available." CME Group said in a statement.

All futures and options contracts on the Globex platform were impacted, with notifications sent to traders around 0300 GMT.

data.title: Asian stocks decline amid tech rally fades
data.description:
Asia-Pacific stocks declined amid fading tech sector gains and a lack of cues as from Thursday's U.S. market closure.

Bitcoin prices have rebounded to $91,477.00 after hitting a 7-month low of $83,517.  

Gold climbed to a two-week-high, with prices trading just below the 4,200 mark. 

Japan (NKY:IND) -0.20% to 48,660 after gaining more than 3% in the last 2 days. Tokyo Core CPI (YoY) for November showed an actual increase of 2.8%, slightly above the forecast of 2.7%, and in-line with prior reading of 2.8%. Additionally, the Jobs/applications ratio for October was reported at 1.18, which is slightly below the forecast of 1.20. The Unemployment Rate for October remained stable at 2.6%, consistent with previous reading, but slightly higher than forecast of 2.5%. Industrial Production (MoM) for October showed a decrease of 1.2%, indicating potential challenges in the manufacturing sector. In terms of retail performance, Large Scale Retail Sales YoY for October reported a growth of 1.7%, surpassing the previous figure of 0.2%. At 12:00 AM ET, Japan will release construction orders and Housing starts activity for October.

Australia (AS51) -0.33% at 8,582. The Housing Credit for October reported a modest increase of 0.6%, matching the previous month's figure. Additionally, the Private Sector Credit for October also saw a rise of 0.7%, slightly above the previous month's growth of 0.6%.

China +0.21% to 3,883.

Hong Kong -0.09% to 25,922. 

U.S. stock futures rose on Friday, with the Dow up 0.17%, S&P 500 gaining 0.15%, and Nasdaq advancing 0.26%, following Wednesday's positive close. U.S. markets were closed yesterday on account of Thanksgiving day.
